es.literal: to be up to the nostrils
es.meaning: To be fed up; to have had enough.
es.example: Estoy hasta las narices de sus excusas.
es.exampleTranslation: I'm fed up with his excuses.
es.regionNote: PENINSULAR. In Mexico the common equivalents are 'estar harto' (neutral) or 'estar hasta la madre' (vulgar — do NOT ship as a neutral equivalent). Needs a proper LatAm variant.

it.literal: to have it up over the hair
it.example: Ne ho fin sopra i capelli di questa situazione.
it.exampleTranslation: I've had it up to here with this situation.

bridge.note: Both measure exasperation by how high it has risen — Spanish to the nose, Italian above the hair.

interference.es_to_it: The Italian partitive ne is doing real work (ne ho fin sopra i capelli) and is a B1 marker Spanish speakers routinely drop.

review.notes: ES side has the sharpest regional problem in the bank — the natural MX equivalent is vulgar. Tag interference_risk added on import to match the ne warning; original had shared_metaphor only.
